evidence: The paper uses "feature importance measures, partial dependence plots, accumulated local effect plots, and the H-statistic" with Figure 1 showing Gini importance rankings ("item format and task emerged as the two most influential predictors") and Figures 2-5 providing visual corroboration via PDP and ALE plots showing actual predicted-probability relationships. The empirical data has minimal predictor correlations (median 0.003), where their own Simulation Study 2 demonstrates importance rankings are relatively reliable; conclusions prioritize effect shapes from PDP/ALE visualizations over magnitude differences in importance scores.
